vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) is present but appears dormant/empty. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only active biological anchor, though it shows signs of severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains soil but no visible foliage. Status: Dormant/Loss.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains soil and eggshell debris. No plant matter detected. Status: Systemic Loss.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows a single, chlorotic, drooping leaf. The stem appears thin and lacks turgor pressure. Status: Critical/Failing.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially hydrophobic or compacted. No signs of moss or secondary growth.
biome_observations.debris: Persistent presence of crushed eggshells in the P3 pot. A small, black, multi-legged electronic component (likely a DIP-package IC) appeared in the T-1 image and remains in the current frame.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic matter or fungal blooms observed.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: P3 shows a gradual loss of leaf turgor. The leaf is curling and losing structural integrity.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant change: The P3 plant has collapsed further. A small electronic component (IC) was introduced to the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: The P3 leaf has become nearly translucent and is in a state of advanced senescence/necrosis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from severe dehydration or root-zone failure, evidenced by the chlorosis and loss of turgor. The absence of P2 and P4 suggests a total failure of the intended botanical arrangement.
anomalies: Introduction of an electronic component (DIP-package IC) near the pots.
Presence of eggshells as a soil amendment, which appears ineffective given the plant decline.
Total absence of the registered Mexican Mint and Silver Guest.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The Pothos in the P3 pot is the only remaining specimen, and it is currently in a state of terminal collapse. The desk surface has been modified by the introduction of non-biological debris (electronic component). The lack of growth and the state of the soil suggest an environment unsuitable for the registered species.
